cwe: CWE-776 - Improper Restriction of Recursive Entity References in DTDs ('XML Entity Expansion')

threat.title: XML Bomb (Billion Laughs) Denial of Service Attack
threat.description: The XML Bomb attack, also known as Billion Laughs, exploits XML parsers' handling of entity expansion to cause exponential memory consumption. Attackers send specially crafted XML documents containing recursive entity references that expand to billions of characters, exhausting server memory and CPU resources, rendering the application unavailable.

attack.scenario:
1. Attacker identifies XML input endpoint (REST API, SOAP service, file upload)
2. Crafts malicious XML with nested entity definitions (e.g., lol0-lol9)
3. Each entity references previous entities multiple times (exponential expansion)
4. Sends XML bomb payload to vulnerable endpoint
5. XML parser begins expanding entities recursively
6. Server memory exhausted (gigabytes consumed from kilobyte payload)
7. Application crashes or becomes unresponsive, denying service to legitimate users
prerequisites: Application accepts XML input without entity expansion limits
XML parser has DTD processing enabled
No resource limits configured on parser
External entity processing enabled (XXE protection not implemented)

cve_references.description: Apache Xerces-C++ XML Parser vulnerable to XML entity expansion (Billion Laughs) attack causing DoS
XStream library vulnerable to Denial of Service via XML entity expansion when processing untrusted XML
Spring Framework vulnerable to DoS via maliciously crafted XML with deeply nested entities causing stack overflow

real_world_breaches.impact: Industry-wide guidance on XML bomb attacks affecting SOAP/REST APIs across financial and healthcare sectors
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Default XML parser configurations allow unlimited entity expansion and external entity processing

real_world_breaches.impact: Federal guidance documenting XML parsing vulnerabilities including entity expansion attacks in web services
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Lack of standardized secure XML parsing configurations across enterprise applications

bug_bounty_reports.summary: XML bomb DoS via SOAP endpoint causing complete service outage

security_research.title: Attacking XML Security: The Quadratic Blowup and Billion Laughs Attacks
security_research.key_finding: XML bombs can expand a 1KB payload to 3GB of memory consumption with just 10 entity definitions

security_research.title: OWASP Testing Guide: XML Entity Expansion
security_research.key_finding: XML bomb attacks remain prevalent in SOAP-based financial and healthcare APIs despite being well-documented since 2002

mitigations.title: Configure Entity Expansion Limits in Spring Boot XML Parsers
mitigations.description: Set strict limits on entity expansion depth, count, and total size in DocumentBuilderFactory and SAXParserFactory to prevent exponential memory consumption. This prevents both recursive entity expansion (Billion Laughs) and excessive entity replacement.
nist_csf: PR.IP-1 - A baseline configuration is created and maintained
PR.PT-5 - Mechanisms are implemented to achieve resilience
validation_steps: Test with billion laughs payload (should reject with SAXException)
Monitor memory usage during XML processing (should remain constant)
Confirm entity expansion limit is set to 0
Test with legitimate XML documents (should parse successfully)

mitigations.title: Disable DTD Processing in ASP.NET Core XML Parsers
mitigations.description: Configure XmlReader and XmlDocument to completely disable DTD processing and entity expansion. Set DtdProcessing to Prohibit and MaxCharactersFromEntities to prevent XML bomb attacks in .NET applications.
nist_csf: PR.DS-5 - Protections against data leaks are implemented
validation_steps: Create unit test with billion laughs XML (should throw XmlException)
Verify DtdProcessing is set to Prohibit
Test MaxCharactersFromEntities limit (reject documents exceeding 1KB entity expansion)
Monitor memory consumption with large XML files (should remain bounded)

mitigations.title: Implement XML Input Validation and Rate Limiting
mitigations.description: Add application-layer validation to check XML size, structure depth, and entity count before parsing. Combine with API rate limiting to prevent sustained DoS attempts. This defense-in-depth approach provides protection even if parser configuration is missed.
nist_csf: PR.IP-1 - A baseline configuration is created
validation_steps: Test with XML containing <!ENTITY> declarations (should reject)
Test deeply nested XML (51+ levels, should reject)
Verify rate limiting: send 11 requests in 1 minute (11th should return 429)
Monitor metrics in Spring Actuator for blocked requests

indicators: Sudden spike in memory consumption during XML processing
CPU usage at 100% on application servers
Slow response times or timeouts on XML endpoints
Unusually small request sizes (KB) causing large memory allocations (GB)
Multiple requests to XML endpoints from same IP in short timeframe
Log entries showing entity expansion warnings or DTD parsing errors

log_queries.query:
// Detect XML bomb attack patterns
AppServiceHTTPLogs
| where UriStem contains "/xml" or ContentType contains "xml"
| where TimeGenerated > ago(5m)
| extend RequestSizeKB = todouble(BytesReceived) / 1024
| extend ResponseTime = todouble(TimeTaken)
| where RequestSizeKB < 10 and ResponseTime > 30000 // Small request, long processing
| summarize 
    RequestCount = count(),
    AvgResponseTime = avg(ResponseTime),
    TotalMemoryMB = sum(RequestSizeKB) 
    by ClientIp, bin(TimeGenerated, 1m)
| where RequestCount > 5 or AvgResponseTime > 60000
| project TimeGenerated, ClientIp, RequestCount, AvgResponseTime, Alert = "Potential XML Bomb Attack"
| order by TimeGenerated desc

index=app_logs sourcetype=spring_boot
("OutOfMemoryError" OR "entity expansion" OR "DOCTYPE" OR "SAXException")
| eval alert_severity=case(
    like(_raw, "%OutOfMemoryError%"), "critical",
    like(_raw, "%entity expansion%"), "high",
    like(_raw, "%DOCTYPE%"), "medium",
    1=1, "low"
  )
| stats count as incident_count, values(source_ip) as attacker_ips by alert_severity, _time
| where incident_count > 3
| eval description="Possible XML bomb DoS attack detected"
| table _time, alert_severity, incident_count, attacker_ips, description

// Detect memory exhaustion from XML processing
metricbeat-* AND (system.memory.actual.used.pct > 0.90 OR process.memory.rss.bytes > 2147483648)
| WHERE event.module: "system" 
| WHERE process.name: ("java" OR "dotnet" OR "spring")
| STATS 
    max_memory = MAX(process.memory.rss.bytes), 
    avg_cpu = AVG(system.cpu.user.pct),
    spike_count = COUNT(*)
  BY host.name, process.name, 5m
| WHERE max_memory > 2147483648 OR avg_cpu > 0.80
| EVAL alert_message = CONCAT("High memory/CPU on ", host.name, " - possible XML DoS")

fields @timestamp, @message, requestId, status, duration
| filter @message like /xml|XML|entity|DOCTYPE/
| filter status >= 500 or duration > 30000
| stats count() as error_count, avg(duration) as avg_duration by bin(5m) as time_window
| filter error_count > 10 or avg_duration > 60000
| sort @timestamp desc

tools.name: Prometheus + Grafana
tools.usage: Monitor JVM heap usage, GC frequency, and request latency for XML endpoints

tools.name: ELK Stack
tools.usage: Centralize application logs and create alerts for OutOfMemoryError and entity expansion warnings

tools.name: AppDynamics / New Relic
tools.usage: Track transaction traces and identify slow XML parsing operations
